response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the résumé spans roughly 2006 to present (~19-20 years), which meets the 15-year minimum. Step 2: Seniority — the role is a director-level IC (Principal Engineer, AI strategy), but the candidate's trajectory is senior/staff product engineer; there is no evidence of org-level technical strategy, advising strategic customers, or influence beyond team scope. Step 3: AI depth — current role includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling (recent, ~1 year), but there is no recognized AI expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, and no AI-related advanced degree. Step 4: Cloud — practical experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, high-throughput microservices) exists but at implementation level, not platform-strategy level. Step 5: Entrepreneurial signal — founding GISCollective is a genuine plus and matches the 'entrepreneurial within larger organizations' preference partially. Step 6: Overall — meets minimum bar on paper, but falls well short of the preferred profile and the strategic/leadership scope the role demands.
strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems
Founder experience (GISCollective) plus open-source contributions and public talks/workshops
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at current employer
concerns: No demonstrated recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products/platforms at meaningful scale
Career has stayed at senior/staff IC level with no evidence of director-level strategy, customer advisory, or organizational influence
AI/cloud experience is recent and implementation-focused; no advanced degree in an AI-related field

key_factors.factor: Limited AI track record vs. role's AI-strategy core
Long, broad engineering experience with entrepreneurial signal

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum qualifications (BS in Computer Science, 15+ years of engineering experience) and shows craftsmanship, breadth, and some entrepreneurial initiative. However, this is a director-level AI-strategy role requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products, and the ability to shape cloud/AI direction for an organization and its strategic customers. The résumé reflects a strong senior/staff product engineer with only about a year of AI-adjacent tooling work and no evidence of strategy, advisory, or leadership at the required altitude, so the fit gap is substantial.
